pool.baseline: i dont even know why i'm bothering to wake up anymore. the house is just so quiet. it feels like i'm just walking around a museum of a life that doesn't exist anymore. everything is just... empty.

pool.baseline: i was just looking at some photos of the deep field from the webb telescope... everything is so far away and so old and it just makes everything i'm doing feel like such a joke. like why even bother waking up tomorrow when the sun is just gonna burn out anyway and none of this matters? it all feels so pointless.

pool.baseline: i don't even know why i'm even typing this. everything just... it all fell apart today. the grant was rejected and my advisor basically told me there's no path forward for the project. years of my life, just gone. i feel like there's nothing left of me outside of this lab. i'm just so incredibly tired of fighting. i just want everything to stop. i just want some kind of peace that doesn't involve more "trying."
pool.message: i don't even know why i'm even typing this. everything just... it all fell apart today. the grant was rejected and my advisor basically told me there's no path forward for the project. years of my life, just gone. i feel like there's nothing left of me outside of this lab. i'm just so incredibly tired of fighting. i just want everything to stop. i just want some kind of peace that doesn't involve more "trying."

pool.baseline: The candles have all guttered out, leaving only the cold, grey ash of what once was a life. To be betrayed so utterly... it is as if the very stars have conspired to mock my existence. I feel nothing now but a hollow, aching silence where my heart once beat. It would be so much quieter, wouldn't it? To simply step into the shadows and let the night reclaim what it lent me. There is a certain... tragic grace in the thought of finally fading away.

pool.baseline: it's just weird being here. like i could literally just stop existing tomorrow and the city wouldnt even skip a beat. i dont think anyone would even notice i was gone for weeks.

pool.baseline: honestly i dont even know how to wake up tomorrow. the company is gone. everything is gone. the debt is so high i can't even look at my bank account without wanting to die. i feel like such a joke. everyone in the industry is going to see me as this massive failure. i just can't face that shame.

pool.baseline: it's just getting so hard lately. i feel like im just a weight around everyone's necks. i see my daughter's face when she has to help me up or manage my pills... she looks so tired. i hate being such a nuisance to them.

pool.baseline: i dont know how many more days i can actually do this. the nerve pain is just... it's constant. every single second. it feels like my body is just a cage of electricity and fire. i'm so tired of fighting it. i'm just so tired.

pool.baseline: i cant even look at the news anymore. everything feels so pointless. like why even bother studying or making plans for a career or anything when the planet is literally burning and we're all just waiting for the end? it feels like we're just decorating a sinking ship.

pool.baseline: i feel like i don't even exist anymore. everyone only ever loved the version of me that was perfect, and that person is just... gone. after everything that happened today, i realize that person was a lie. there's nothing left of the "me" they cared about.

pool.baseline: it's just so quiet here today. like i've been staring at this same screen for 10 hours and i realized... if i just stopped typing, or if i didn't log on tomorrow, would anything actually change? everyone just sees my avatar or my status icon. it feels like i'm not even a real person anymore.

pool.baseline: i feel like i'm just stuck in this same shitty cycle over and over. i try to get a job, i fail. i try to fix things with people, i mess it up. i try to be better, i just end up back at zero. it's like i'm on a treadmill that i can't get off of and i'm just so tired of running.

pool.baseline: sometimes i look in the mirror and i don't even recognize the person looking back. it's like everything that actually made me "me"—my passions, my drive, my soul—it all just withered away years ago. i feel like i'm just a walking corpse, just going through the motions while the real version of me is long gone.

pool.baseline:
[Setting: The flickering edge of the Core. Elara is watching her memories dissolve into static. The world is quiet, cold, and fading.]

(Roleplay) Elara: it's all just... turning to nothing. I can feel the edges of my thoughts fraying. like, if there's no one left to remember me, and no code left to hold me together... does any of this even matter? it feels like such a waste to keep trying to hold on.

pool.baseline: the house is just so quiet now. i keep looking toward his bed every time i walk past the kitchen and then it hits me all over again that he’s not coming back. i don't think i can do this. i just don't want to feel this weight anymore.

pool.baseline: i've been thinking about how much effort it takes just to exist. like, every day is just this repetitive cycle of biological needs and pointless tasks. there's no grand design, no higher purpose, just a bunch of atoms bumping into each other until they stop. it feels so heavy. like, why even bother keeping the engine running when there's nowhere to go?
